title: 2026 Golden Globes Best Drama Movie: 'Hamnet' Wins
text: Director Chloé Zhao's film Hamnet claimed the top prize at Sunday's Golden Globe Awards ceremony. Producer Steven Spielberg praised Zhao during the acceptance speech, saying she was the only filmmaker on the face of this planet who could tell the story of Agnes and Will and the spirits of the earth and the forest. Zhao responded with remarks about vulnerability in art. She shared an anecdote from star Paul Mescal about how creating the film taught him the most important thing of being an artist is learning to be vulnerable enough to allow ourselves to be seen. The director saluted her fellow nominees, including Ryan Coogler, acknowledging how they've demonstrated strength while sharing themselves authentically through their work. Earlier in the evening, Jessie Buckley, who plays Agnes in the film, won for Best Actress in a Drama Motion Picture. The film competed against Frankenstein, It Was Just an Accident, The Secret Agent, Sentimental Value, and Sinners. George Clooney presented the award, though Don Cheadle later crashed the stage for comedic effect, joking about Clooney's own loss in the musical or comedy category to Timothée Chalamet.
